name: graphql-schema
description: GraphQL queries, mutations, and code generation patterns. Use when creating GraphQL operations, working with Apollo Client, or generating types.
capabilities: Create .gql files for queries and mutations
Generate TypeScript types from GraphQL operations
Use generated Apollo hooks with proper error handling
Implement optimistic updates for instant UI feedback
author: ChrisWiles
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation and metadata. No executable code, network calls, file access, or command execution. Static findings are false positives triggered by markdown code blocks, content hashes, and documentation patterns. The skill provides GraphQL development guidance only.
valueStatement: Writing GraphQL operations manually leads to type errors and maintenance headaches. This skill provides proven patterns for queries, mutations, and Apollo Client integration with automatic type generation.
limitations: Does not execute GraphQL operations or connect to servers
Requires existing GraphQL schema and codegen setup
Does not write code to your project files
Pattern knowledge only - implementation requires developer action

useCases.title: Set up GraphQL queries
useCases.description: Create type-safe GraphQL queries with generated Apollo hooks for React components

useCases.title: Add mutations with error handling
useCases.description: Implement mutations with required error handlers, loading states, and cache updates

useCases.title: Automate type generation
useCases.description: Generate TypeScript types from .gql files to eliminate manual type maintenance

promptTemplates.title: Create a query
promptTemplates.scenario: Need to fetch data from GraphQL
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a GraphQL query for fetching items with pagination. Include the .gql file and how to use the generated hook.

promptTemplates.title: Create a mutation
promptTemplates.scenario: Need to create or update data
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I create a GraphQL mutation for creating a new item? Include error handling and cache update patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Optimistic updates
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to implement optimistic updates for a toggle mutation so the UI updates instantly before the server responds.

promptTemplates.title: Error handling
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the requirements for error handling in GraphQL mutations? Show a complete pattern with logging and user feedback.

bestPractices: Never inline gql literals - use separate .gql files for maintainability
Always run codegen after modifying .gql files to update types
Add onError handlers to all mutations with user feedback
Use generated hooks instead of raw Apollo hooks
antiPatterns: Inline gql strings in component files - creates hard-to-maintain code
Skipping error handlers on mutations - users see silent failures
Using raw useMutation instead of generated hooks - loses type safety
Not disabling buttons during mutations - allows duplicate submissions

faq.question: Does this skill connect to my GraphQL server?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides patterns and guidance only. You need to configure Apollo Client separately to connect to your server.

faq.question: What GraphQL clients are supported?
faq.answer: Patterns target Apollo Client. Similar concepts apply to other clients like URQL or Relay with minor API differences.

faq.question: What codegen tools work with these patterns?
faq.answer: Apollo Codegen, GraphQL Code Generator, and similar tools generate types from .gql files. The skill assumes codegen is configured.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill is purely prompt-based with no code execution or file access. It cannot read or transmit any data.

faq.question: Why should I use .gql files instead of inline gql?
faq.answer: Separate .gql files enable code generation, improve IDE support, make refactoring easier, and keep components clean.

faq.question: How is this different from writing raw GraphQL?
faq.answer: This skill promotes type-safe generated hooks that catch errors at compile time. Raw GraphQL requires manual type definitions.
